top_strategic.title: The Brand's Own Idea Lab (White Label)
top_strategic.evidence: Sell Nike their own instance of our Idea Lab analyzing ONLY clips where their products appear. Nike uploads competitor videos, our system finds gaps: 'Your shoes underrepresented in backcourt angles' or 'Opponent's racket featured 3x more in viral moments.' Nike pays $200K/year, gets insights they can't get anywhere else, and becomes dependent on our data infrastructure.

top_strategic.title: Brand Bidding Arena
top_strategic.evidence: Flip the model: brands pay to COMPETE for shelf space in our clips. Instead of us detecting Nike in the background, Nike, JOOLA, and On bid in real-time for product placement in future Courtana videos. Facility owners get a cut. The highest bidder's gear gets featured in the next match at that court.

top_strategic.title: The Idea Lab as SaaS for Any Sports Facility Network
top_strategic.evidence: Package the Idea Lab as a $5K/month white-label product for regional sports facility chains (not just pickleball). A chain with 20 multi-sport courts gets an Idea Lab analyzing all their footage autonomously. We become the hidden OS for facility intelligence. Each facility pays us; we own the data; we expand to basketball, tennis, etc.

top_strategic.title: Facility as Media Studio (Revenue Share)
top_strategic.evidence: Stop selling clips. Partner with facilities to become mini ESPN studios. We handle all editing/commentary/distribution; they keep 70% of ad revenue + sponsorship deals we broker. A facility with Courtana becomes a media asset. We scale to 100 facilities = 100 revenue streams. Each facility is incentivized to host bigger tournaments.

top_strategic.title: Coaching Certification Authority
top_strategic.evidence: Use our AI to certify pickleball coaches. Coaches submit videos of their instruction; we analyze shot breakdown, communication clarity, drill design. Certified coaches get a badge, can charge 3x more, and must use Courtana courts to maintain certification. We become the permissioning layer for professional coaching.

top_data.title: DaaS badge prediction: F1=0.97 (precision=0.98, recall=0.96)
top_data.evidence: Across 287 verified clips: 999 correct predictions, 16 false positives, 41 missed badges. Overall F1=0.972.
top_data.action: Target recall improvement: add more badge examples to Gemini prompt

top_data.title: Prompt fix: 5 badges consistently missed — add positive examples
top_data.evidence: Production awards these but DaaS misses them: 'rally master' (6 missed), 'new look' (5 missed), 'steady eddie' (4 missed), 'fresh fit' (3 missed), 'paddle tap celebration' (3 missed). These are real badges that our prompt doesn't detect.
top_data.action:
Add positive examples to Gemini prompt:
'When analyzing badges, make sure to check for these commonly missed badges:
  - rally master: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - new look: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - steady eddie: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - fresh fit: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - paddle tap celebration: Look for [describe visual pattern]

top_data.title: Net/venue branding inflates 1 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
top_data.evidence: Brands with significant venue-only detections (not product use): JOOLA (195/1931 are venue). Raw counts overstate market share. Product-use only: JOOLA=1736, Nike=198, PICKLEBALL=77, HEAD=45, PICKLEBALL KINGDOM=24.
top_data.action: Report TRUE share-of-court (product-use only) to brand partners, not raw detection counts

top_data.title: Viral formula: quality (7.59 vs 5.42) + watchability (6.45 vs 3.14)
top_data.evidence: Top 100 viral clips: quality=7.59, watchability=6.45. Bottom 100: quality=5.42, watchability=3.14. Both metrics track strongly with viral potential.
top_data.action: Use (quality + watchability) / 2 as quick viral prediction score

top_data.title: Worst predicted badges: serve returner (F1=0.875), hustler (F1=0.769)
top_data.evidence: Lowest F1 badges: serve returner (F1=0.875), hustler (F1=0.769). These need prompt improvement.
top_data.action: Add specific examples for these badge types in Gemini prompt

all_findings.title: Crowdsourced Coaching Marketplace (Viral Clips → Coaching Gigs)
all_findings.evidence: When a player's clip hits a viral threshold, we auto-match them with 3-5 coaches in their region. Coaches bid to coach that player. The player chooses; we take 20%. Players get better → play more matches → more clips → more coaching gigs. Each coach becomes a node in a viral loop.
all_findings.action: Evaluate: crowdsource play

all_findings.title: Prompt fix: 5 badges consistently missed — add positive examples
all_findings.evidence: Production awards these but DaaS misses them: 'rally master' (6 missed), 'new look' (5 missed), 'steady eddie' (4 missed), 'fresh fit' (3 missed), 'paddle tap celebration' (3 missed). These are real badges that our prompt doesn't detect.
all_findings.action:
Add positive examples to Gemini prompt:
'When analyzing badges, make sure to check for these commonly missed badges:
  - rally master: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - new look: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - steady eddie: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - fresh fit: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - paddle tap celebration: Look for [describe visual pattern]

all_findings.title: Sponsorship whitespace: 5 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
all_findings.evidence: The camera sees these items but we detect no brands. ZERO detections: accessories, apparel, footwear, sunglasses, wristband. Near-zero: hat(5). Each gap = a brand that could be paying for visibility data.
all_findings.action: Add shoe/apparel/sunglasses brand detection examples to Gemini prompt — immediate revenue unlock

all_findings.title: Player-Owned Highlight NFTs
all_findings.evidence: Each clip becomes a fractional NFT that players, brands, and facilities can own pieces of. When a clip goes viral or gets licensed, royalties auto-distribute to all holders. Players are incentivized to play at Courtana courts (to build their highlight portfolio), facilities get ongoing micro-revenue, and brands sponsor specific players' highlight collections.
all_findings.action: Evaluate: network_effect play

all_findings.title: Net/venue branding inflates 1 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
all_findings.evidence: Brands with significant venue-only detections (not product use): JOOLA (195/1931 are venue). Raw counts overstate market share. Product-use only: JOOLA=1736, Nike=198, PICKLEBALL=77, HEAD=45, PICKLEBALL KINGDOM=24.
all_findings.action: Report TRUE share-of-court (product-use only) to brand partners, not raw detection counts

all_findings.title: Worst predicted badges: serve returner (F1=0.875), hustler (F1=0.769)
all_findings.evidence: Lowest F1 badges: serve returner (F1=0.875), hustler (F1=0.769). These need prompt improvement.
all_findings.action: Add specific examples for these badge types in Gemini prompt

all_findings.title: Prompt fix: JOOLA appears in 215/287 verified clips — add disambiguation
all_findings.evidence: JOOLA dominates 75% of clips. Prompt should distinguish: paddle logo (real JOOLA use), net logo (JOOLA sponsors nets), and venue branding (JOOLA sponsors the facility).
all_findings.action:
Add to brand detection prompt:
'For JOOLA specifically, classify detection source:
  - paddle: JOOLA logo on paddle face = confirmed product use
  - net: JOOLA logo on net = venue sponsorship, NOT product use
  - venue: JOOLA on walls/banners = facility sponsorship
Only count paddle detections as brand_category: paddle'

all_findings.title: Sponsorship whitespace detected in 973 clips (88.5%) — sell to brands
all_findings.evidence: We detect unbranded equipment/space in 973 clips. This is the gap between what's visible and what's branded — sellable data.
all_findings.action: Package sponsorship whitespace data as 'brand opportunity report' for equipment companies

all_findings.title: Viral shot: 'overhead' clips average 5.8 viral vs 'serve' at 1.25
all_findings.evidence: Shot viral averages: overhead=5.8, volley=4.19, dink=3.22, drive=3.09, serve=1.25
all_findings.action: Prioritize highlight reels featuring high-viral shot types

all_findings.title: Viral arc: 'top_play' is 130.0x more likely in top clips (13.0% vs 0.0%)
all_findings.evidence: Story arc 'top_play' appears in 13.0% of top-viral clips but only 0.0% of bottom-viral clips.
all_findings.action: Prioritize 'top_play' clips in automated highlight reels

all_findings.title: Endorsement intelligence: 61 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
all_findings.evidence: Top pairs: PickleBill-JOOLA(223x), Tmf-JOOLA(145x), Ironvarr-JOOLA(133x), Santinofuntila-JOOLA(130x), David_220cfb17-JOOLA(103x)
all_findings.action: Build 'brand ambassador identification' product for equipment companies

all_findings.title: Brand-skill split: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM users excel at kitchen (7.5), R&M_foz_wifi_goomba users at power (5.1)
all_findings.evidence: Among brands with 10+ clips: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM has highest avg kitchen mastery (7.5), while R&M_foz_wifi_goomba has highest power rating (5.1).
all_findings.action: Build brand-skill correlation report for brand partnerships

all_findings.title: Longitudinal tracking possible: 26 players with 5+ clips
all_findings.evidence: 26 players have enough data for improvement tracking. Top: PickleBill(111), Santinofuntila(63), Ironvarr(62), Tmf(59), David_220cfb17(34)
all_findings.action: Build player improvement trajectory product — track skill changes over time
